behaviors.description: Scanner extracts all direct and transitive dependencies from manifest files (package.json, requirements.txt, Pipfile, etc.)
behaviors.justification: Real manifest files and realistic dependency resolution are required to catch parsing bugs and incomplete transitive extraction. Mocking the parser and resolver would hide failures in actual implementations where lock file parsing fails or transitive deps are dropped.
behaviors.test_description: Create test manifests with direct and transitive dependencies, execute scanner, verify extracted list matches all expected dependencies including transitive ones from lock files
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Common failure: transitive dependencies are skipped during lock file parsing or resolution stops early. Must use real manifest and lock file formats.

behaviors.description: Vulnerability database API query includes exact version ranges from manifest, and results contain only CVEs affecting those versions rather than all versions of the package
behaviors.justification: Query construction logic must be tested with realistic API validation to catch version-range omissions or malformed range syntax. A mocked API without strict query validation would pass even if version ranges are absent or incorrectly formatted.
behaviors.test_description: Mock vulnerability database with strict validation of query format, scan manifest with specific version ranges, verify queries include version constraints and returned CVEs only match those ranges
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Failure mode: scanner queries without version constraints (e.g., queries 'react' instead of 'react@^17.0.0'), receiving 10,000+ irrelevant CVEs. Mock must validate query structure strictly to catch this.

behaviors.description: Vulnerability report output is sorted by severity (Critical > High > Medium > Low), with same-severity CVEs sorted by CVSS score descending
behaviors.justification: Pure sorting logic with no I/O, external dependencies, or dynamic behavior. Sorting bugs are caught at the logic level without filesystem, network, or subprocess involvement. No external system state or non-determinism.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test with parametrized CVE lists: unsorted mixed-severity items with various CVSS scores, verify output list is sorted by severity first, then CVSS score within each severity tier
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Straightforward deterministic business logic. Test the comparator function directly with fixture data, no mocking needed.

behaviors.description: When bumping a dependency version in the manifest, only that dependency's entry is modified; all other dependencies remain unchanged and manifest file remains valid after serialization
behaviors.justification: Manifest parsing and serialization bugs only surface when reading/writing real files. Mocking file I/O would hide corruption bugs, accidental removal of entries, or reordering during serialization. Format-specific parsers (JSON, YAML, TOML) must work correctly.
behaviors.test_description: Create test manifests in real formats (JSON/YAML), call scanner to bump one dependency, read modified file from disk, verify bumped dependency has new version, all others unchanged, manifest parses without error
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Common failure: manifest reordering when serializing, or accidental removal of entries. Must test with actual file I/O and real parser implementations.

behaviors.description: Auto-generated pull request is created on real repository with correct single-dependency diff; only the bumped dependency version appears in the PR diff, no unrelated changes
behaviors.justification: Creating a PR on the real repository requires actual git operations (commit, push) and PR API calls. Integration tests with mocked PR API miss API-side failures. The specification states 'real repository', requiring System-level testing with real git operations and PR infrastructure.
behaviors.test_description: Create test repository, scanner bumps dependency and attempts PR creation, verify PR exists on real repository with correct git diff showing only the bumped dependency version change
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Real-world failure: PR includes accidental whitespace changes, manifest reordering, or incorrect git operations due to subprocess/command bugs. Must use real git and PR API (or test instance) to catch.

behaviors.description: Major version bumps (e.g., 1.0.0 → 2.0.0) are correctly identified as breaking changes based on semantic versioning comparison rules
behaviors.justification: Pure semver comparison logic with no external dependencies or I/O. Test the version parser and comparator directly with fixture version pairs. No state, no non-determinism, no mocking needed.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test with parametrized semver pairs: (1.0.0 → 2.0.0) is breaking, (1.0.0 → 1.1.0) is not, (1.0.0 → 1.0.1) is not; verify comparator categorizes each correctly
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Logic-only test. Use standard semver library, verify correct semantic versioning rules applied.

behaviors.description: When breaking-change version bump is detected, pull request creation is skipped and a review flag is surfaced in scanner output to alert the user
behaviors.justification: Must verify output format contains review flag and PR creation is skipped. Unit tests with mocks would miss output formatting bugs or flag placement issues. Integration tests the complete flow: detect breaking change → skip PR API call → generate and verify output with flag.
behaviors.test_description: Load manifest with breaking version bump, run scanner, verify PR creation API call is not made, verify output contains visible review flag with correct format and placement
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test should mock PR API to verify skipped call, but use real output serialization to catch formatting bugs. Failure mode: flag present but invisible to downstream tools, or placed in wrong section of output.
